changes.value:
## What

In `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, the CM6 extension array (lines 318–371) registers extensions in this order:

1. `keymapCompartment.current.of(keymapExtension(mode))` — vim/emacs extension
2. `keymap.of([...])` — Enter, ArrowUp, ArrowDown
3. `EditorView.domEventHandlers({...})` — Escape handler

CM6 calls `domEventHandlers` in extension-precedence order (first registered = highest priority). Because the vim extension is first, it consumes Escape in normal mode (clearing pending operators, exiting visual mode, etc.) **before** the dismiss handler ever fires. The user must click the backdrop or press Escape outside CM6 focus to dismiss.

The same issue may affect emacs mode if `@replit/codemirror-emacs` consumes Escape internally.

**Fix**: Move the `EditorView.domEventHandlers` Escape handler to be the **first** extension in the array, giving it highest precedence. The existing logic already correctly checks vim state:
- `insertMode` → return `false` (let vim exit insert mode)
- normal mode → call `onClose()`, return `true` (dismiss palette)

Also extend the vim state check to cover `visualMode` / `visualLine` / `visualBlock` so Escape exits those modes before dismissing.

**Files to modify**:
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx` — reorder extensions array
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.test.tsx` — add test for vim normal-mode dismiss

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pressing Escape in vim normal mode dismisses the command palette
- [ ] Pressing Escape in vim insert mode still exits to normal mode (does not dismiss)
- [ ] Pressing Escape in CUA mode dismisses the command palette (regression check)
- [ ] Pressing Escape in emacs mode dismisses the command palette

## Tests
- [ ] Add test in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.test.tsx`: "vim normal mode: Escape closes palette" — mock getCM to return `{ state: { vim: {} } }` (no insertMode), simulate Escape keydown on the CM6 editor view, assert `onClose` called
- [ ] Add test: "vim insert mode: Escape does NOT close palette" — mock getCM to return `{ state: { vim: { insertMode: true } } }`, simulate Escape, assert `onClose` NOT called
- [ ] Run `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` — all tests pass
Fix: Escape key does not dismiss command palette in vim normal mode (or emacs)

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,32 +1,28 @@
 ## What

-In `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, the CM6 extension array (lines 318–371) registers extensions in this order:
-
-1. `keymapCompartment.current.of(keymapExtension(mode))` — vim/emacs extension
-2. `keymap.of([...])` — Enter, ArrowUp, ArrowDown
-3. `EditorView.domEventHandlers({...})` — Escape handler
+In `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, the CM6 extension array (lines 318–371) uses a hand-rolled `EditorView.domEventHandlers` for Escape, registered after the vim extension. The vim extension consumes Escape in normal mode before the dismiss handler fires.

-CM6 calls `domEventHandlers` in extension-precedence order (first registered = highest priority). Because the vim extension is first, it consumes Escape in normal mode (clearing pending operators, exiting visual mode, etc.) **before** the dismiss handler ever fires. The user must click the backdrop or press Escape outside CM6 focus to dismiss.
+The codebase already has `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/cm-submit-cancel.ts`) — a shared helper that correctly handles vim Escape via a two-phase capture/bubble DOM listener strategy. `text-editor.tsx` and `filter-editor.tsx` already use it.

-The same issue may affect emacs mode if `@replit/codemirror-emacs` consumes Escape internally.
+**Fix**: Replace the ad-hoc `EditorView.domEventHandlers` Escape handler and the `keymap.of([Enter, ArrowUp, ArrowDown])` block with `buildSubmitCancelExtensions`. The palette's contract:
+- **onSubmit** (Enter) → execute selected command/result
+- **onCancel** (Escape) → `onClose()` (dismiss palette)
+- `singleLine: true`

-**Fix**: Move the `EditorView.domEventHandlers` Escape handler to be the **first** extension in the array, giving it highest precedence. The existing logic already correctly checks vim state:
-- `insertMode` → return `false` (let vim exit insert mode)
-- normal mode → call `onClose()`, return `true` (dismiss palette)
-
-Also extend the vim state check to cover `visualMode` / `visualLine` / `visualBlock` so Escape exits those modes before dismissing.
+Arrow key navigation (ArrowUp/ArrowDown) stays as a separate `keymap.of` since that's palette-specific, not submit/cancel.

 **Files to modify**:
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx` — reorder extensions array
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.test.tsx` — add test for vim normal-mode dismiss
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx` — replace lines 318–371 with `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` + arrow key keymap
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.test.tsx` — add test for vim normal-mode Escape dismiss

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] Pressing Escape in vim normal mode dismisses the command palette
-- [ ] Pressing Escape in vim insert mode still exits to normal mode (does not dismiss)
-- [ ] Pressing Escape in CUA mode dismisses the command palette (regression check)
-- [ ] Pressing Escape in emacs mode dismisses the command palette
+- [ ] Pressing Escape in vim insert mode exits to normal mode (does not dismiss)
+- [ ] Pressing Escape in CUA/emacs mode dismisses the command palette
+- [ ] Enter still executes the selected command
+- [ ] ArrowUp/ArrowDown still navigate the list

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Add test in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.test.tsx`: "vim normal mode: Escape closes palette" — mock getCM to return `{ state: { vim: {} } }` (no insertMode), simulate Escape keydown on the CM6 editor view, assert `onClose` called
-- [ ] Add test: "vim insert mode: Escape does NOT close palette" — mock getCM to return `{ state: { vim: { insertMode: true } } }`, simulate Escape, assert `onClose` NOT called
+- [ ] Add test in `command-palette.test.tsx`: "vim normal mode: Escape closes palette"
+- [ ] Add test: "vim insert mode: Escape does NOT close palette"
 - [ ] Run `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` — all tests pass
\ No newline at end of file
